MOTION / Amended Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Seal Records
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ENERSPONSE, LLC's Motion To Seal Records.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to seal business records filed under seal in connection with Enersponse's motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication is granted. The court finds that there is an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records; this overriding interest supports sealing the records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. As set out in the moving papers, the sealed materials would reveal financial terms of customer agreements. This information is kept confidential by Enersponse and Enersponse's factual showing that its publication would subject Enersponse to competitive harm is unchallenged.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SMARTRENT TECHNOLOGIES,INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION Motion To Seal

The unopposed motion of SmartRent Technologies, Inc. ("SmartRent") to seal portions of the motion for summary judgment lodged conditionally under seal by Entersponse, LLC, on January 28, 2025 is granted. The court orders the following material permanently sealed: 1. Portions of Exhibit N to the Declaration of Proud Usahacharoenporn in Support of Enersponse's MSJ ("Usaha Declaration"), specifically (a) Exhibit 16 to the transcript of Justin Lesko ("Lesko Transcript") and related deposition testimony at lines 179:19-180:13; and (b) Exhibit 17 to the Lesko Transcript and related deposition testimony at lines 180:14-181:25; 2. Exhibit P to the Usaha Declaration; 3. Exhibit V to the Usaha Declaration; 4. Exhibit K to the Declaration of James McPhail in Support of Enersponse's MSJ ("McPhail Decl.") and paragraph 20 of the McPhail declaration; and 5. The following portions of Enersponse's memorandum of points and authorities in support of its MSJ, which discuss the contents of the exhibits above: (a) page 16, lines 3-16; (b) page 17, lines 4-6 and lines 15-21; and (c) page 23, lines 10-14.

With respect to these materials, the court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. The court finds pursuant to SmartRent's moving papers and supporting declaration that this material contains secret information about SmartRent's business and internal business evaluations, disclosure of which is likely to threaten its competitive abilities and harm its customer relationships.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Seal Records Conditionally Filed Under Seal (Amended)
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ENERSPONSE, LLC's Motion To Seal Records Conditionally Filed Under Seal.

The unopposed motion of Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Enersponse, LLC ("Enersponse") for an order maintaining under seal certain portions of Defendant/Cross-Complainant SmartRent Technologies, Inc.'s ("SmartRent") Motion for Summary Judgment ("Motion"), specifically portions of SmartRent's: (1) Memorandum of Points and Authorities in support of the Motion ("Memorandum"); (2) Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in support of the Motion ("Separate Statement"); and (3) Declaration of James Maxwell Cooper in support of the Motion, including Exhibits G - M, O, and P attached thereto, and Exhibit Nos. 49, 50, 51 to James McPhail's deposition (Exhibit P) (collectively, the"Confidential Exhibits") is granted.

The court orders as follows: The exhibits presently lodged by SmartRent provisionally under seal in connection with its summary judgment motion are hereby permanently sealed. Enersponse shall attach Exhibit 1 to the declaration of Marcel Budiono to the written order it prepares for this court so that those lesser redacted versions of Exhibits O and P to the Copper Declaration, filed in connection with SmartRent's summary judgment motion, shall be placed in the public court file with the filing of this order.

The court finds that, for the reasons set out in Enersponse's moving papers, supported by declaration showing the proprietary and confidential nature of the materials affected by this order, (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
